title: Exact Duplicates, Boilerplate, Similarity, Canonicals, and Change Detection · Behavior Case: Canonical Content Separation
summary: Tracking variants duplicate pages, shared navigation dominates hashes, a canonical hint suppresses evidence, and a similarity score is treated as proof of sameness. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the canonical content separation increment in content identity and change-evidence model. Learners progress through prediction, ex…
objectives: Treat rel=canonical, redirects, normalized URL keys, exact content, and near duplicates as separate signals that may disagree.
Use documented tokenization, fingerprints, thresholds, and labeled evaluation and report uncertain similarity without claiming semantic equality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-canonical-content-separation — Treat rel=canonical, redirects, normalized URL keys, exact content, and near duplicates as separate signals that may disagree.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-content-dedup-change-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-content-dedup-change-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_content_dedup_change_5(fetched_pages: int = 80, exact_groups: int = 7, near_groups: int = 5, changed_pages: int = 18, unsupported_equivalence_claims: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if fetched_pages < 1 or exact_groups < 0 or near_groups < 0:
        return False, fetched_pages, "dedup-count"
    if changed_pages < 0 or changed_pages > fetched_pages:
        return False, changed_pages, "change-count"
    if unsupported_equivalence_claims > 0:
        return False, unsupported_equivalence_claims, "similarity-overclaim"
    if exact_groups + near_groups < 2:
        return False, exact_groups + near_groups, "dedup-evidence"
    assert changed_pages <= fetched_pages and unsupported_equivalence_claims == 0
    return True, changed_pages, "bounded-content-identity"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Canonical Content Separation model · Theory Canonical Con…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Canonical Content Separation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 5285 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate byte, source, text, record, canonical, similarity, boilerplate, and change evidence; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat rel=canonical, redirects, normalized URL keys, exact content, and near duplicates as separate signals that may disagree.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Treat rel=canonical, redirects, normalized URL keys, exact content, and near duplicates as separate signals that may disagree.
